metadata.title: NaturaRef Yoga Asana Library
metadata.subtitle: Therapeutic Yoga Reference for BNYS Clinical Practice
metadata.author: NaturaRef — Dr. Vikranth BNYS

instructions: Stand erect with feet together, big toes touching, heels slightly apart.
Distribute body weight evenly on all four corners of each foot.
Engage thigh muscles, lift kneecaps without locking knees.
Lengthen the tailbone toward the floor, engage the core gently.
Roll shoulders back and down; chest broad, arms by sides, palms facing forward.
Elongate the neck, chin parallel to floor, gaze forward.
Breathe steadily for 30–60 seconds.

respiratory: Opens chest, improves breathing capacity
digestive: Stimulates digestive awareness through core engagement
nervous: Calms the mind, improves concentration and body awareness
cardiovascular: Mild improvement in circulation through lower limbs

modifications.elderly: Stand with feet hip-width apart; use wall for support if balance is impaired.
modifications.obese: Feet hip-width apart to reduce strain on ankles and knees.
modifications.beginners: Practice near a wall; place fingers lightly on wall for balance.
modifications.pregnancy: After first trimester, widen stance to hip-width; avoid closed-eye variation.

instructions: Begin in Tadasana. Shift weight to the left foot.
Bend right knee, place right foot on inner left thigh (above or below knee, never on knee joint).
Press foot and thigh into each other for stability.
Bring palms together at chest (Anjali mudra) or raise arms overhead.
Fix gaze on a stationary point (drishti) at eye level.
Hold for 30–60 seconds, breathing steadily.
Release and repeat on opposite side.

musculoskeletal: Strengthens ankles, calves, thighs, and spine
respiratory: Encourages diaphragmatic breathing

absolute: severe arthritis of hip or knee
relative: recent ankle sprain — use lower foot placement

modifications.elderly: Place foot on inner calf instead of thigh; keep fingertips on wall.
modifications.obese: Place foot on inner calf or ankle; use chair back for support.
modifications.beginners: Stand near wall; start with toe touching floor for balance.
modifications.pregnancy: Use wall support; avoid deep hip rotation in third trimester.

instructions: Stand with feet 3–3.5 feet apart; right foot out 90°, left foot in 15°.
Arms extended parallel to floor at shoulder height.
Inhale; on exhale, extend trunk sideways over right leg, hinging at hip.
Place right hand on shin, ankle, or block (not forcing).
Extend left arm straight up, palm forward; gaze toward raised hand.
Keep both sides of the trunk equally long; do not collapse chest.
Hold 30–60 seconds. Inhale to come up, repeat left side.

musculoskeletal: Stretches hamstrings, groins, hips, IT band
Relieves neck and low back stiffness
respiratory: Opens intercostal muscles, improves lateral chest expansion
cardiovascular: Improves circulation in lower limbs

absolute: acute low back pain — avoid deep lateral bend
relative: cervical spondylosis — keep neck neutral (do not look up)

modifications.elderly: Use a block under lower hand; reduce range of motion.
modifications.obese: Use tall block or chair seat under lower hand.
modifications.beginners: Place lower hand on shin or use block; focus on trunk lengthening over depth.
modifications.pregnancy: Widen stance; use block support; avoid compressing belly.

instructions: From Tadasana, step left foot back 4–4.5 feet.
Rotate left foot in 45–60° inward; right foot points forward.
Square hips to face front as much as possible.
Bend right knee to 90° (knee over ankle, not beyond toes).
Raise arms overhead, palms facing each other or joined.
Lift chest, lengthen spine; slight backbend if comfortable.
Hold 20–45 seconds. Return to Tadasana, repeat other side.

musculoskeletal: Improves hip mobility and spinal extension
respiratory: Opens chest and rib cage, improves lung capacity
endocrine: Stimulates adrenal response, builds energy
cardiovascular: Increases heart rate mildly; good warm-up for cardiovascular system

relative: shoulder impingement — keep hands on hips

modifications.elderly: Shorten stance; use wall for balance; keep arms on hips if shoulder issues.
modifications.obese: Shorter stance; hands on thighs for support.
modifications.beginners: Use wall at back for balance; shorten lunge depth.
modifications.pregnancy: Shorten stance; avoid deep lunge in third trimester; keep hands on hips.

instructions: Stand with feet 4–4.5 feet apart.
Right foot out 90°, left foot in 15°; align right heel with left arch.
Extend arms out to sides at shoulder height, parallel to floor.
Bend right knee to 90°; keep knee tracking over second toe.
Turn head to gaze over right fingertips.
Keep trunk upright (do not lean forward); shoulders relaxed.
Hold 30–60 seconds. Straighten leg, repeat left side.

respiratory: Expands chest, improves breathing
cardiovascular: Good cardiovascular conditioning with regular practice

modifications.elderly: Shorten stance; use chair for partial support.
modifications.obese: Shorten stance to reduce knee strain.
modifications.beginners: Reduce bend depth; do not force knee to 90°.
modifications.pregnancy: Widen stance; remove arm variation if shoulder fatigue.

instructions: Stand in Tadasana. Inhale, raise arms overhead, palms facing each other.
Exhale, bend knees as if sitting back into a chair.
Thighs as parallel to floor as possible; knees tracking over feet.
Trunk slightly forward of vertical; back lengthened, chest lifted.
Keep weight in heels; you should be able to lift toes.
Hold 20–40 seconds with steady breathing. Inhale, straighten legs. Exhale, lower arms.

respiratory: Expands chest; improves respiratory muscle tone
endocrine: Stimulates metabolic rate
cardiovascular: Raises heart rate; good cardiovascular conditioning

modifications.elderly: Partial squat only; use chair back for support.
modifications.obese: Hold chair back; reduce depth of bend; ensure knees do not go beyond toes.
modifications.beginners: Stand near wall; lightly press back against wall to learn alignment.
modifications.pregnancy: After first trimester, stand wider; use wall support; avoid deep squat.

instructions: Stand in Tadasana. Inhale, extend arms overhead.
Exhale, hinge forward at hips (not waist), leading with chest.
Place hands on floor, blocks, or shins — wherever they land with a straight spine.
Allow head to hang; relax neck completely.
With each exhale, fold deeper; knees may be slightly bent initially.
Hold 30–60 seconds. To come up: inhale, hands on hips, rise with flat back.

musculoskeletal: Relieves lumbar stiffness and disc compression (decompresses L4-S1)
respiratory: Calms nervous system when head is below heart
digestive: Stimulates liver, kidneys, spleen; aids constipation
cardiovascular: Mild inversion effect; calms heart rate

relative: glaucoma — head should not go below heart level

modifications.elderly: Bend knees generously; use blocks under hands.
modifications.obese: Use blocks; bend knees widely; avoid compressing abdomen.
modifications.beginners: Bend knees to learn hip hinging; place hands on blocks.
modifications.pregnancy: Not suitable in second/third trimester; use wide-leg variation only.

instructions: Begin in Trikonasana on right side.
Bend right knee, bring right hand to floor 12 inches ahead of right foot.
Shift weight onto right foot and hand; lift left leg parallel to floor.
Open hips; stack left hip over right. Extend left arm toward ceiling.
Gaze up toward left hand or straight ahead if balance is challenging.
Hold 20–30 seconds. Lower left foot to floor, return to Trikonasana. Repeat left.

respiratory: Opens chest and shoulders

modifications.elderly: Use wall for back support; keep gaze forward.
modifications.obese: Use block under lower hand; stay near wall.
modifications.beginners: Use block at highest height; wall support.
modifications.pregnancy: Not recommended in second/third trimester.

instructions: Sit on folded blanket or mat. Cross legs comfortably in front of you.
Each foot rests beneath opposite knee; shins crossed.
Hands on knees, palms down (Jnana mudra or Chin mudra).
Sit erect; lengthen spine; relax shoulders down and back.
Close eyes, breathe naturally. Alternate crossing of legs at each sitting.

respiratory: Ideal base for pranayama and breathing practices
nervous: Calms mind, reduces anxiety and mental fatigue

relative: acute knee injury — use Dandasana instead
severe hip arthritis — use chair adaptation

modifications.elderly: Sit on chair; feet flat on floor; hands on thighs.
modifications.obese: Use thick folded blanket under hips to elevate pelvis.
modifications.beginners: Sit with back against wall; use blanket under hips.
modifications.pregnancy: Safe throughout; increase blanket height as pregnancy progresses.

instructions: Sit in Dandasana (legs extended). Bend right knee, bring right foot to left hip crease — sole facing up.
Bend left knee, lift left foot onto right thigh — sole facing up.
Both knees should ideally touch or near the floor.
Spine erect; hands in mudra on knees. Close eyes, breathe naturally.
Do not force — hip flexibility must be adequate before attempting.

respiratory: Optimal posture for diaphragmatic breathing and pranayama
cardiovascular: Reduces blood pressure with regular meditative practice

modifications.elderly: Use Sukhasana; do not attempt full lotus with stiff joints.
modifications.obese: Not appropriate; use Sukhasana or chair sitting.
modifications.beginners: Ardha Padmasana (half lotus) first; progress over months.
modifications.pregnancy: Sukhasana preferred; full lotus contraindicated after first trimester.

instructions: Kneel on mat, knees together, toes pointing back.
Lower hips to sit on heels; tops of feet flat on floor.
Sit erect with hands on thighs, palms down.
Keep spine straight; relax shoulders; breathe normally.
This is the only asana that can be practiced immediately after meals.

digestive: Significantly improves digestion — increases blood flow to pelvic and abdominal region
Reduces gas, bloating, acidity, IBS symptoms
urogenital: Strengthens pelvic floor; beneficial in urinary disorders

absolute: severe knee arthritis or recent knee surgery
relative: pregnancy (third trimester) — may compress leg circulation

modifications.elderly: Place folded blanket between calves and thighs; use thicker padding under knees.
modifications.obese: Use thick blanket or block between hips and feet to reduce knee strain.
modifications.beginners: Fold blanket and place between calves and thighs for comfort.
modifications.pregnancy: Safe in first trimester; second trimester use blanket support; avoid in third trimester.

instructions: Sit in Dandasana. Bend knees, bring soles of feet together, heels close to perineum.
Grasp feet or ankles with both hands.
Sitting tall, inhale; exhale, gently allow knees to lower toward floor.
Do not force knees down — inner groin releases passively over time.
Optionally flap knees gently like butterfly wings for dynamic variation.
Fold forward from hips (not waist) for deeper stretch.

musculoskeletal: Opens hip joints and sacroiliac region
endocrine: Stimulates ovaries and prostate gland
cardiovascular: Improves circulation in pelvic basin
urogenital: Highly therapeutic for menstrual disorders, menopausal symptoms
Assists in urinary tract health
Benefits prostate conditions

absolute: acute groin or inner knee injury
relative: knee injury — support knees with blankets

modifications.elderly: Use blankets or blocks under each knee for support.
modifications.obese: Sit on blanket; allow knees to stay high — do not force.
modifications.beginners: Sit on blanket to elevate hips; support knees with blocks.
modifications.pregnancy: Very beneficial; use blanket under knees; no forward fold with belly.

instructions: Sit in Dandasana; legs together, fully extended.
Inhale, raise arms overhead, lengthen spine.
Exhale, hinge from hips (not waist) and fold forward.
Grasp feet, ankles, or shins — wherever you can without rounding the lower back excessively.
With each inhale, lengthen the spine; with each exhale, fold slightly deeper.
Hold 1–3 minutes. To exit: inhale, raise head and chest, uncurl spine.

musculoskeletal: Intense stretch for hamstrings, calves, lumbar spine
respiratory: Calms respiratory rate
endocrine: Stimulates adrenal glands and pancreas — beneficial for diabetes
nervous: Reduces anxiety, fatigue; therapeutic in mild depression
cardiovascular: Lowers blood pressure with regular practice
urogenital: Tones kidneys; beneficial for urinary health

relative: pregnancy — avoid from second trimester onward

modifications.elderly: Bend knees generously; use strap around feet; hold shins.
modifications.obese: Use strap around feet; bend knees; no forced forward fold.
modifications.beginners: Use strap; focus on hinging from hips not rounding the lower back.
modifications.pregnancy: Contraindicated from second trimester; use wide-leg adaptation only.

asanas.english: Half Lord of the Fishes / Half Spinal Twist
instructions: Sit in Dandasana. Bend left knee; place left heel beside right hip.
Cross right foot over left leg and place it flat outside the left knee.
Inhale and lengthen the spine.
Exhale, twist to the right — left elbow against right knee as a lever.
Right hand behind the right hip; gaze over right shoulder.
Keep spine tall throughout. Breathe freely for 30–60 seconds.
Inhale to unwind; repeat on opposite side.

musculoskeletal: Stretches outer hips, chest, shoulders, neck
respiratory: Alternates lung compression for improved respiratory capacity
digestive: Massages abdominal organs — liver, kidneys, stomach, spleen
Stimulates digestive juices; excellent for constipation and IBS
endocrine: Massages adrenal glands and pancreas — key for diabetes management
nervous: Tones spinal nerves and reduces nerve root compression
urogenital: Stimulates kidneys; beneficial for mild renal conditions

modifications.elderly: Sit on blanket; reduce twist depth; keep lower leg extended.
modifications.obese: Keep lower leg extended (Marichyasana variation); reduce twist intensity.
modifications.beginners: Keep lower leg extended; use light hand placement not deep lever.
modifications.pregnancy: Very gentle; avoid deep twist compressing abdomen; use open-belly variation.

instructions: Sit in Dandasana. Bend right knee, place right sole against inner left thigh.
Inhale, lengthen spine. Exhale, hinge forward from hips over left (extended) leg.
Grasp left foot, ankle, or use strap. Keep left leg straight.
With each exhale, elongate torso over leg; do not round lower back severely.
Hold 1–2 minutes. Return upright. Repeat with right leg extended.

musculoskeletal: Stretches hamstrings, calves, inner thigh of bent leg
respiratory: Mild chest compression on exhale
digestive: Stimulates liver, kidneys, spleen on forward side
endocrine: Stimulates adrenal glands
nervous: Calming; reduces mild anxiety and depression
cardiovascular: Lowers blood pressure
urogenital: Benefits kidney function

modifications.elderly: Use strap; slight knee bend in extended leg.
modifications.obese: Use strap; bend extended knee generously.
modifications.beginners: Use strap around foot; focus on hip hinge.
modifications.pregnancy: Widen base; use strap; avoid from second trimester.

instructions: Sit with right leg crossed over left; stack right knee directly above left knee.
Feet pointed back on either side of hips; sit evenly on both sitting bones.
Raise right arm overhead, bend elbow, reach right hand down the back.
Bring left arm behind the back from below; reach left hand up.
Clasp fingers (use strap if hands don't meet).
Sit tall; breathe steadily. Hold 1–2 minutes. Release and repeat opposite side.

musculoskeletal: Deep stretch for outer hips, IT band, piriformis, triceps, chest
respiratory: Opens chest; improves shoulder mobility for better breathing mechanics
urogenital: Beneficial for kidney and renal function

modifications.elderly: Use strap between hands; reduce hip crossing.
modifications.obese: Sit on blanket; avoid full leg crossing if hip restriction present.
modifications.beginners: Use strap; legs may not stack perfectly — allow difference.
modifications.pregnancy: Arm variation only; skip leg crossing in second/third trimester.

instructions: Sit in Dandasana. Bend knees, feet flat on floor.
Lean back slightly, lift feet off floor; shins parallel to floor (half boat).
Extend arms forward at shoulder height, parallel to floor.
For full boat: straighten knees, legs at 45° — forming a V shape.
Spine long, chest lifted — do not round excessively.
Engage core and hip flexors. Hold 15–30 seconds.

musculoskeletal: Strengthens core (rectus abdominis, transversus abdominis), hip flexors
endocrine: Stimulates thyroid
cardiovascular: Stimulates heart function
urogenital: Tones kidney and prostate

modifications.elderly: Half boat only (knees bent); use hands on floor for support.
modifications.obese: Half boat; hands on floor behind hips; do not force leg straightening.
modifications.beginners: Half boat with bent knees; work up to straight legs gradually.
modifications.pregnancy: Contraindicated.

instructions: Stand with feet wider than hip-width, toes pointing out 45°.
Bend knees deeply into a squat; lower hips toward floor.
Bring palms together at chest; press elbows into inner thighs to open knees.
Keep spine long; heels on floor if possible (use blanket under heels if tight).
Hold 1–3 minutes, breathing steadily.

musculoskeletal: Deeply opens hip joints, groins, ankles
digestive: Excellent for constipation; stimulates bowel peristalsis
urogenital: Opens pelvic floor; beneficial for PCOS, menstrual health
Supports pelvic organ prolapse prevention

relative: ankle tightness — use blanket under heels
knee arthritis — use partial squat with chair support

modifications.elderly: Use chair for support; sit on edge of chair in squat-like position.
modifications.obese: Use two blocks under sitting bones; hold chair for balance.
modifications.beginners: Place blanket under heels; use wall for balance.
modifications.pregnancy: Very beneficial — excellent for labour preparation; use wall support.

instructions: Lie prone (face down). Feet together, toes pointing back.
Place palms flat on floor beside lower ribs; elbows close to body.
Inhale slowly; press hands gently and lift head, neck, chest off floor using back muscles primarily.
Elbows may be slightly bent or straightened based on flexibility.
Shoulders rolled back and down; pubic bone presses into floor.
Hold 15–30 seconds. Exhale and lower slowly.

musculoskeletal: Strengthens entire spine, erector spinae muscles
Relieves low back pain and kyphosis
respiratory: Opens chest wall; excellent for asthma and bronchitis
Improves lung volume and tidal breathing
endocrine: Stimulates adrenal glands; mild thyroid stimulation
nervous: Energising; reduces fatigue and mild depression

relative: pregnancy — avoid from first trimester onward

modifications.elderly: Sphinx variation (forearms on floor); minimal height.
modifications.obese: Sphinx variation; avoid full cobra; limit to low baby cobra.
modifications.beginners: Baby cobra (small lift); focus on spinal muscle engagement over hand push.
modifications.pregnancy: Contraindicated; use Marjaryasana instead.

instructions: Lie prone. Arms along body, palms facing down or up.
Forehead on floor initially. On inhale, lift head, chest, arms and both legs simultaneously.
Firm glutes; press pubic bone into mat.
Arms stretched back; gaze forward and slightly upward.
Hold 10–30 seconds. Exhale, lower everything slowly.
Progress: begin with alternate leg lifts, then full pose.

musculoskeletal: Strongly strengthens the entire posterior chain: glutes, hamstrings, erector spinae
Corrects kyphosis, reduces lumbar disc compression
respiratory: Strengthens respiratory muscles; opens anterior chest
endocrine: Stimulates adrenal glands
urogenital: Tones pelvic floor and lower abdominal organs

absolute: acute lumbar disc herniation with radiculopathy

modifications.elderly: Alternate legs only; no full simultaneous lift.
modifications.obese: Alternate legs; use pillow under abdomen for support.
modifications.beginners: Alternate leg lifts first; add arm lifts; full pose over weeks.
modifications.pregnancy: Contraindicated.

instructions: Lie prone. Bend both knees; reach back and grasp ankles (not toes).
Inhale; simultaneously kick legs into hands while lifting chest off floor.
Body rests on abdomen; head lifted, gaze forward.
Thighs and chest should be equally lifted off floor.
Breathe steadily — the rocking motion on the abdomen massages organs.
Hold 15–30 seconds. Exhale, release ankles, lower slowly.

musculoskeletal: Full spinal extension; opens anterior body completely
respiratory: Massages lungs; opens chest for asthma, bronchitis
digestive: Abdominal pressure massage — stimulates liver, pancreas, intestines
Highly effective for constipation and obesity
endocrine: Stimulates pancreas — excellent for diabetes management
Thyroid stimulation

modifications.elderly: Use strap around ankles; lift only one leg at a time (Ardha Dhanurasana).
modifications.obese: Ardha Dhanurasana (half bow, one leg at a time); do not force.
modifications.beginners: Ardha Dhanurasana first; ensure adequate shoulder and quadricep flexibility.
modifications.pregnancy: Contraindicated.

instructions: Lie prone. Place arms in front of body in a V-shape; rest forehead on crossed forearms.
OR: forehead on stacked hands. Legs apart, toes out or in (as comfortable).
Close eyes; completely relax entire body.
Observe natural breath; allow belly to expand into floor on each inhale.
The crocodile position activates the diaphragm optimally for belly breathing.

musculoskeletal: Relieves acute and chronic low back pain naturally
respiratory: Best position for diaphragmatic breathing retraining
Reduces hyper-inflation in asthma and COPD
nervous: Deep relaxation of parasympathetic nervous system
Reduces anxiety, hypertension via breath-body link
cardiovascular: Lowers blood pressure with regular relaxed breathing

relative: pregnancy — second/third trimester avoid prone position; use left lateral
breast tenderness — use pillow under chest

modifications.elderly: Pillow under abdomen for comfort; blanket under feet.
modifications.obese: Pillow under chest and head; legs wider.
modifications.beginners: Ideal as a rest and breathing pose; no modification needed.
modifications.pregnancy: First trimester only; use pillow support.

instructions: Begin on hands and knees. Bring big toes together; knees apart or together.
Sit hips back toward heels (as far as comfortable).
Extend arms forward on mat OR arms alongside body, palms up.
Forehead rests on mat. Completely surrender and relax.
Breathe into the back of the body — feel the lower back expand.

musculoskeletal: Gently stretches low back, sacrum, hips, thighs
respiratory: Posterior lung breathing; excellent for COPD and anxiety
nervous: Deep calming of the nervous system; reduces anxiety attacks
Safe rest pose for beginners and elderly
cardiovascular: Reduces blood pressure

relative: pregnancy — use wide-knee variation with bolster

modifications.elderly: Wide knee variation; use folded blanket under hips.
modifications.obese: Wide knee variation; bolster between thighs and chest.
modifications.beginners: Ideal rest pose; no concern about depth.
modifications.pregnancy: Wide-knee variation with bolster under chest — safe and comfortable.

instructions: Begin on hands and knees (tabletop position); wrists under shoulders, knees under hips.
COW (Bitilasana): Inhale, drop belly toward floor, lift chest and tailbone — gentle backbend. Gaze forward.
CAT (Marjaryasana): Exhale, round the spine toward ceiling, drop head, tuck tailbone — C-curve.
Flow between these two positions with each breath — 10–20 cycles.
Keep movement initiated from the tailbone and rippling up through the spine.

musculoskeletal: Mobilises entire spinal column — all segments including cervical
Warms up the spine for more intensive practice
respiratory: Coordinates breath with movement — retrains respiratory awareness
nervous: Calming and grounding; synchronises nervous system

relative: acute wrist injury — use fists or forearms
severe cervical disc herniation — minimal neck movement

modifications.elderly: Seated on chair — alternate rounding and arching back.
modifications.obese: Standard position; wrist blocks if wrist discomfort.
modifications.beginners: Ideal warm-up pose; emphasise breath coordination.
modifications.pregnancy: Excellent — safe throughout pregnancy; reduces back pain.

instructions: Begin in tabletop. Tuck toes; press into palms — spread fingers wide.
Exhale, lift knees off floor; push hips up and back, forming an inverted V.
Straighten legs (or keep slight bend in knees); press heels toward floor.
Head between arms, ears aligned with upper arms; gaze toward navel.
Arms strong, shoulders externally rotated — do not let arms cave inward.
Hold 30–90 seconds with steady breathing.

musculoskeletal: Stretches hamstrings, calves, shoulders, arches of feet
respiratory: Mild inversion — calms respiration
nervous: Energising yet calming; reduces fatigue and mild depression
cardiovascular: Mild inversion benefits — calms heart rate

modifications.elderly: Hands on chair seat instead of floor; 45° angle.
modifications.obese: Use chair; feet wider; do not force heel-to-floor.
modifications.beginners: Bend knees generously; focus on spinal length over leg straightening.
modifications.pregnancy: Dolphin variation (forearms) or hands-on-wall version.

instructions: Lie on back. Legs slightly apart, feet falling out naturally.
Arms slightly away from body, palms facing up.
Close eyes. Allow the entire body weight to surrender into the floor.
Systematically relax from feet upward: toes, feet, calves, knees, thighs, hips, abdomen, chest, hands, arms, shoulders, neck, face, scalp.
Observe breath passively. Remain still for 5–20 minutes.
To exit: gently wiggle fingers and toes; roll to right side; sit up slowly.

respiratory: Deepens diaphragmatic breathing
Reduces respiratory rate
endocrine: Lowers cortisol and adrenaline
Balances ANS
nervous: Most profound relaxation pose — directly activates PNS
Reduces anxiety, PTSD symptoms, chronic pain
cardiovascular: Reduces heart rate and blood pressure
Essential in hypertension management

relative: severe depression with withdrawal tendencies — use brief Savasana only
late pregnancy — use left lateral position instead

modifications.elderly: Pillow under head and knees for comfort.
modifications.obese: Pillow under knees; arms slightly elevated on blanket.
modifications.beginners: Eye pillow helps reduce distraction.
modifications.pregnancy: Left lateral Savasana (use bolster between knees) from second trimester.

instructions: Lie on back. Inhale; on exhale, bring right knee to chest and clasp with both hands.
Gently press knee toward right shoulder; extend left leg flat on floor.
Hold 30–60 seconds. Release and repeat with left knee.
Both knees variation: bring both knees to chest simultaneously; clasp both knees.
Optionally rock gently side to side for spinal massage.
Advanced: lift head and touch chin or forehead to knee(s).

musculoskeletal: Stretches lower back, sacrum, hip flexors
digestive: Highly effective for gas, bloating, flatulence
Stimulates large intestine — excellent for constipation
urogenital: Massages reproductive organs

relative: pregnancy — single leg variation only in first trimester

modifications.elderly: Single leg; gentle pressure; pillow under head.
modifications.obese: Single leg variation; use belt if arms can't reach around knee.
modifications.beginners: Single leg; no head lift initially.
modifications.pregnancy: Single leg only; extremely gentle in first trimester only.

instructions: Lie on back. Bend knees, feet flat on floor hip-width apart, close to hips.
Arms alongside body, palms flat on floor.
Inhale; press feet into floor and lift hips toward ceiling.
Roll shoulders under; clasp hands under back for a stronger lift.
Keep thighs parallel; knees over ankles — do not let them splay outward.
Hold 30–60 seconds. Exhale, unroll spine back to floor vertebra by vertebra.

respiratory: Opens chest; improves lung capacity
endocrine: Stimulates thyroid (neck extension)
Mild stimulation of pituitary
nervous: Calming; reduces anxiety and mild depression
cardiovascular: Mild inversion benefit
urogenital: Strengthens pelvic floor; beneficial in urinary incontinence

relative: shoulder injury — use hands flat instead of clasped

modifications.elderly: Use block under sacrum for supported bridge; less effort.
modifications.obese: Supported bridge with block under sacrum; feet wider.
modifications.beginners: Supported bridge with block is excellent introduction.
modifications.pregnancy: Supported bridge with block; after second trimester avoid flat back if dizzy.

instructions: Lie on back. Lift legs to 90°. Support back with palms.
Exhale, swing legs overhead until toes touch the floor behind the head (or hover if tight).
Arms can stay supporting back or extend along floor.
Keep neck relaxed — do not turn head; weight on shoulders, not neck.
Hold 1–5 minutes. To exit: slowly roll down, vertebra by vertebra.

musculoskeletal: Intense posterior chain stretch — entire spine, hamstrings
respiratory: Stimulates thyroid and parathyroid glands
digestive: Strong stimulation of abdominal organs, liver, spleen
endocrine: Strong thyroid and parathyroid stimulation
cardiovascular: Semi-inversion; beneficial for venous return

modifications.elderly: Supported Halasana — toes on chair behind head; reduced spinal load.
modifications.obese: Not recommended; use Viparita Karani instead.
modifications.beginners: Let toes rest on chair or block; keep support of hands on back.
modifications.pregnancy: Contraindicated.

instructions: Lie on back. Bring legs to 90°, then lift hips off floor with hand support on lower back.
Continue lifting until body is vertical, resting on upper shoulders and back of neck.
Hands support mid-back; elbows on floor, close together.
Gaze at chest; do not turn the head.
Keep neck in natural position — do not press neck into floor.
Hold for 2–10 minutes with diaphragmatic breathing.
Lower slowly: bend knees, unroll spine to floor.

musculoskeletal: Tones the entire body — the 'mother of all asanas'
respiratory: Improves venous drainage of head and thorax
digestive: Reverses abdominal organ position — relieves constipation, prolapsed conditions
endocrine: Very strong thyroid and parathyroid stimulation — key for hypothyroidism
nervous: Deeply calming; major effect on parasympathetic dominance
cardiovascular: Reduces heart rate; improves cardiac efficiency; beneficial in mild heart conditions
urogenital: Reverses pelvic organ prolapse tendencies; benefits uterine and ovarian health

modifications.elderly: Supported Sarvangasana against wall; use folded blankets under shoulders.
modifications.obese: Not recommended; use Viparita Karani instead.
modifications.beginners: Blankets stacked under shoulders to protect cervical spine; use wall initially.
modifications.pregnancy: Contraindicated.

instructions: Sit sideways against a wall. As you lie back, swing legs up the wall.
Hips close to or touching wall; legs straight up (or slightly bent).
Arms out to sides, palms up. Close eyes and breathe naturally.
A folded blanket under hips creates a more restorative inverted angle.
Hold 5–20 minutes. To exit: bend knees, push off wall, roll to side.

respiratory: Calms the nervous system and respiratory rate
endocrine: Mild thyroid and endocrine stimulation
cardiovascular: Drains venous pooling from lower limbs — excellent for varicose veins, oedema
Reduces blood pressure
urogenital: Beneficial for menstrual disorders, PCOS when combined with breathing

absolute: serious venous blood clot (DVT) — risk of dislodgement
relative: menstruation — avoid for heavy flow (some traditions)
hypertension — may use without bolster under hips

modifications.elderly: Excellent for elderly; blanket under hips optional; fully supported.
modifications.obese: Safe and beneficial; blanket under hips helps.
modifications.beginners: Easiest restorative pose — highly recommended.
modifications.pregnancy: Not recommended from second trimester onward; use left lateral.

instructions: Lie on back. Bring right knee to chest; extend left leg flat.
On exhale, drop right knee across body to left side; left hand guides knee down gently.
Extend right arm to the right at shoulder height; gaze toward right hand.
Keep right shoulder grounded; let gravity do the work.
Hold 1–3 minutes. Inhale to return to centre. Repeat on left side.

musculoskeletal: Releases paraspinal muscles; mobilises thoracic and lumbar spine
Stretches IT band, piriformis, outer hip
respiratory: Alternating chest compression retrains thoracic breathing
digestive: Stimulates peristalsis; relieves constipation and IBS
endocrine: Massages adrenal glands
nervous: Deeply calming; reduces sympathetic nervous activity

modifications.elderly: Place pillow under dropped knee for support.
modifications.obese: Block or pillow under rotated knee.
modifications.beginners: Ideal restorative twist; no complex modifications needed.
modifications.pregnancy: Not safe from second trimester onward.

instructions: Lie on back, legs extended. Slide hands under hips, palms facing down.
Inhale; press elbows into floor and lift chest high, arching the back.
Tilt head back and gently rest crown of head on floor.
Chest, not head, carries the weight. Keep legs active.
Breathe deeply into the chest — the pose should expand breathing capacity.
Hold 30–60 seconds. Press elbows, lift head, lower back to floor.

musculoskeletal: Stretches entire anterior body — neck, throat, chest, abdomen
respiratory: Maximally opens chest and intercostals — excellent for asthma
Stretches trachea and throat
endocrine: Strong thyroid stimulation — one of best poses for hypothyroidism
Stimulates parathyroid
cardiovascular: Opens anterior chest wall; good for mild cardiac conditions

modifications.elderly: Supported fish — use bolster under thoracic spine; no deep neck extension.
modifications.obese: Supported fish with bolster; head on small pillow.
modifications.beginners: Keep head gently on floor; do not force deep neck extension.
modifications.pregnancy: Contraindicated.

instructions: Kneel; interlace fingers on mat; place crown of head on floor between palms.
Forearms on floor form a stable tripod.
Tuck toes; lift hips and walk feet toward head until hips stack over shoulders.
Engage core; slowly lift both legs toward ceiling — straight and together.
Hold with steady breath; gaze at fixed point; full body engaged.
Lower slowly; rest in Balasana for the same duration as the headstand.

respiratory: Reversal of lung circulation — improves respiratory function
digestive: Reverses gravitational pull on intestines — relieves constipation, prolapse
endocrine: Maximum pituitary and pineal gland stimulation
Regulates hormonal system
cardiovascular: Reduces cardiac workload — reduces BP in legs
Improves cerebral circulation

modifications.elderly: Contraindicated for elderly without prior experience; use Viparita Karani.
modifications.obese: Contraindicated; use Viparita Karani.
modifications.beginners: Only under direct supervision; use wall; master Dolphin pose first.
modifications.pregnancy: Contraindicated.

instructions: Kneel hip-width apart; knees and feet hip-width apart, tops of feet flat on floor.
Place hands on lower back, fingers pointing down; elbows drawing together.
Inhale, lift chest high; begin to arch back.
Reach hands to heels one at a time (tuck toes for easier access).
Push hips forward; lift chest toward ceiling; head drops back if no neck issues.
Hold 20–40 seconds. To exit: hands to lower back, engage core, rise chin last.

musculoskeletal: Deep spinal extension; stretches entire anterior body
respiratory: Major chest opener — excellent for asthma, bronchitis, kyphosis
endocrine: Thyroid stimulation
cardiovascular: Opens cardiac region

modifications.elderly: Hands on lower back only; no reaching to heels.
modifications.obese: Hands on lower back; no heel reach; minimal arch.
modifications.beginners: Tuck toes to raise heel height; use blocks beside feet.
modifications.pregnancy: Not recommended from first trimester.

instructions: Lie on back. Bend knees, feet flat, hip-width. Bend elbows, place palms beside ears.
Inhale; press into hands and feet; lift hips, trunk, and shoulders off floor.
Straighten arms; roll onto crown of head briefly; then fully extend arms.
Hold with strong arms, legs, and engaged core.
Hold 10–30 seconds. Lower slowly to head, then back to floor.

musculoskeletal: Strengthens wrists, arms, shoulders, core, thighs, glutes
respiratory: Maximally opens thorax and chest
endocrine: Stimulates thyroid, adrenal glands

modifications.elderly: Not appropriate; use supported bridge instead.
modifications.obese: Not recommended.
modifications.beginners: Master bridge and camel first; never attempt without guidance.
modifications.pregnancy: Contraindicated.

instructions: From tabletop, step feet back until body is in one straight line.
Arms straight, wrists under shoulders; spread fingers wide.
Engage core, thighs, glutes; body straight from head to heels.
Do not let hips sag or pike up.
Breathe steadily. Hold 15–60 seconds.

musculoskeletal: Core (deep and superficial), arms, shoulders, chest, legs all strengthened simultaneously
cardiovascular: Mild cardiovascular conditioning

modifications.elderly: Knee plank (modify on knees); build toward full plank.
modifications.obese: Knee plank initially; forearm plank reduces wrist stress.
modifications.beginners: Knee plank; forearm plank.
modifications.pregnancy: Not recommended after first trimester.

instructions: Sit on mat with legs fully extended together in front.
Sit on sitting bones; flex feet (toes pointing to ceiling).
Palms flat on floor beside hips; fingertips pointing forward.
Sit upright; elongate spine; do not lean back.
Engage quadriceps; press thighs into floor.
Breathe normally and hold 1–2 minutes.

musculoskeletal: Foundation pose — teaches correct sitting alignment
respiratory: Encourages upright breathing

relative: tight hamstrings — sit on blanket to tilt pelvis forward

modifications.elderly: Sit on blanket; use wall for back support.
modifications.obese: Sit on firm blanket; slight bend in knees acceptable.
modifications.beginners: Blanket under hips; wall support.
modifications.pregnancy: Safe; use blanket under hips.

instructions: Kneel, knees together. Widen feet slightly beyond hip-width.
Slowly lower hips to sit between (not on) the feet.
Hands on thighs or in mudra. Spine upright.
If hips don't reach floor, sit on a block between feet.
Hold 2–5 minutes; alternate with Vajrasana.

modifications.elderly: Block between feet for hip support; padded surface under knees.
modifications.obese: Block under hips mandatory; padded mat.
modifications.beginners: Block between feet; blanket under ankles.
modifications.pregnancy: Use block; safe in first trimester.

instructions: Sit in Dandasana. Open legs wide apart — as wide as comfortable without strain.
Flex feet; engage thighs; knee caps facing ceiling.
Inhale, lengthen spine. Exhale, hinge from hips and walk hands forward.
Aim chin, then chest toward floor — do not round the lower back.
Hold 1–3 minutes with steady breath.

musculoskeletal: Intense inner thigh and hamstring stretch
digestive: Stimulates kidney function; beneficial in kidney disorders
urogenital: Stimulates ovaries and uterus
Beneficial for menstrual disorders, PCOS

relative: low back injury — minimal forward fold
pregnancy — use bolster between legs and torso

modifications.elderly: Sit on blanket; walk hands only slightly forward.
modifications.obese: Sit on blanket; reduce leg opening angle.
modifications.beginners: No deep fold — work on leg opening width first.
modifications.pregnancy: Wide leg variation with bolster under chest.

instructions: Lie on back. Bring soles of feet together; let knees fall open to sides.
Arms out to sides, palms up. Close eyes.
Use bolsters, blankets, or blocks under knees for support.
Allow hips and inner thighs to release passively.
Breathe deeply; 3–10 minutes.

musculoskeletal: Passive deep groin and hip opening
respiratory: Opens chest in reclined position — very calming breath
endocrine: Stimulates reproductive organs
nervous: Excellent for hormonal balance via PNS activation
cardiovascular: Reduces blood pressure
urogenital: Highly therapeutic for menstrual pain, PCOS, endometriosis
Benefits prostate

relative: knee injury — use higher blanket supports under knees

modifications.elderly: Generous blanket support under both knees.
modifications.obese: Blocks or rolled blankets under each knee.
modifications.beginners: Use blocks or blankets — do not force knees toward floor.
modifications.pregnancy: Excellent — use bolster under back and blankets under knees.

instructions: Stand in Tadasana. Bend knees slightly; lift right foot and cross right thigh over left.
Hook right foot behind left calf if possible, or rest right foot beside left ankle.
Cross right arm under left at elbow level; wrap forearms; press palms together.
Squeeze elbows and knees toward midline; lift elbows to shoulder height.
Gaze at a fixed point; hold 30–60 seconds. Unwind and repeat other side.

musculoskeletal: Strengthens and stretches ankles, calves, hips, thighs
Opens upper back and scapular region
respiratory: Opens rhomboid region between scapulae — helpful for kyphosis
urogenital: Loosens legs — beneficial for varicose veins, urinary disorders

modifications.elderly: Wall support; foot rest against ankle rather than wrapped.
modifications.obese: Wall support; arm variation only if balance difficult.
modifications.beginners: Wall support; focus on one element at a time (leg first, then arm).
modifications.pregnancy: Not recommended.

instructions: Stand, step feet wide apart (4–5 feet). Feet parallel or slightly turned in.
Hands on hips. Inhale, lengthen spine. Exhale, hinge from hips forward.
Place hands on floor between feet (shoulder-width); top of head toward floor.
Work toward placing crown of head on floor between hands.
Hold 30–60 seconds. Inhale, hands on hips, come up slowly.

cardiovascular: Mild inversion benefit

relative: glaucoma — keep head above heart level

modifications.elderly: Hands on blocks; minimal depth.
modifications.obese: Hands on blocks; reduced angle.
modifications.beginners: Blocks under hands; emphasis on hip hinge.
modifications.pregnancy: Not recommended from second trimester; head should not invert.

instructions: Begin in Virasana. Place hands behind you.
Lean back slowly, first onto forearms, then fully recline onto the floor.
Arms overhead or alongside body. Breathe naturally.
If knees are uncomfortable, place bolster under back.
Hold 1–5 minutes. Use arms to press up to sitting.

respiratory: Opens anterior chest and ribs

relative: tight quadriceps — use bolster under back

modifications.elderly: Do not attempt without Virasana mastery; use bolster.
modifications.obese: Not recommended without supervision.
modifications.beginners: Use bolster under back; remain on elbows.
modifications.pregnancy: Contraindicated.

instructions: Begin on forearms; elbows shoulder-width apart; press palms flat.
Tuck toes; lift hips high into Dolphin pose.
Walk feet toward head; kick one leg up at a time to invert.
Stack hips over shoulders; engage core; toes toward ceiling.
Hold with steady breath. Lower carefully.

musculoskeletal: Improves thoracic extension and spinal strength
respiratory: Chest and shoulder opener
nervous: Builds confidence; reduces anxiety over time
cardiovascular: Inversion cardiac benefits

modifications.elderly: Not appropriate; use Viparita Karani.
modifications.obese: Not appropriate.
modifications.beginners: Dolphin pose preparation; wall support mandatory.
modifications.pregnancy: Contraindicated.

instructions: Sit in Sukhasana or Padmasana. Spine erect.
Right hand in Vishnu mudra: fold index and middle finger; use thumb and ring finger.
Close right nostril with thumb; inhale through left for 4 counts.
Close both nostrils (kumbhaka) — optional for beginners, hold for 2 counts.
Release right nostril; exhale through right for 8 counts.
Inhale right for 4 counts; hold 2 counts; exhale left for 8 counts.
This is ONE cycle. Repeat 5–20 cycles.

respiratory: Balances right and left nasal passages
Strengthens respiratory musculature
Beneficial for asthma, sinusitis, rhinitis
endocrine: Balances ANS-endocrine axis
nervous: Balances sympathetic and parasympathetic nervous systems
Most effective single technique for reducing anxiety and stress
cardiovascular: Reduces blood pressure significantly with regular practice
Improves heart rate variability

modifications.elderly: Shorter ratios; no kumbhaka; seated in chair.
modifications.obese: Chair seated; no kumbhaka initially.
modifications.beginners: Start with 4:0:4 ratio (no retention); progress to 4:2:8.
modifications.pregnancy: Safe without kumbhaka; very beneficial.

instructions: Sit comfortably with spine erect.
Take a deep inhale through both nostrils.
Begin short, sharp, forceful exhalations through the nose, pulled by rapid abdominal contractions.
Inhalation is passive — it happens automatically after each sharp exhale.
Rhythm: 1–2 exhalations per second. Start with 30 strokes.
After a round, exhale fully and breathe normally for 1 minute before the next round.

respiratory: Clears bronchial tubes and upper airways
Strengthens diaphragm and intercostal muscles
Excellent for asthma, COPD (sub-acute)
digestive: Massages abdominal organs — liver, spleen, kidneys, intestines
endocrine: Stimulates pancreas — beneficial for diabetes
Activates metabolism significantly
cardiovascular: Improves circulation

modifications.elderly: Slow rhythm (1 per 2 seconds); short duration; no vigorous contractions.
modifications.obese: Moderate pace; start with 20 strokes per round.
modifications.beginners: 30 strokes at slow rhythm; build over weeks.
modifications.pregnancy: Contraindicated.

instructions: Sit comfortably. Close eyes.
Raise arms; close ears with thumbs; index fingers on forehead; middle fingers on closed eyes; ring fingers below nostrils; little fingers on lips (Shanmukhi mudra) — OR simply close ears with thumbs.
Inhale deeply through nostrils.
Exhale slowly making a humming sound like a bee — 'mmmmm' — deep and resonant.
Feel the vibration in the skull, throat, and chest.
5–15 rounds.

respiratory: Lengthens exhalation — activates vagal brake
Useful for rhinitis and sinusitis
endocrine: Stimulates pituitary gland via cranial vibration
nervous: Most effective for instant anxiety and panic relief
Beneficial in Alzheimer's and cognitive decline
cardiovascular: Reduces heart rate and BP rapidly

absolute: ear infection — do not close ears with thumbs
relative: epilepsy — avoid prolonged breath retention variant

modifications.elderly: Simply close ears with thumbs; no mudra needed; shorter sessions.
modifications.obese: Chair seated; very accessible.
modifications.beginners: Start with 5 rounds; simply close ears with thumbs.
modifications.pregnancy: Excellent — safe; no modifications needed.

instructions: Sit comfortably or use during asana practice.
Slightly constrict the glottis (back of throat) as if fogging a mirror — same constriction for inhalation and exhalation.
Both inhale and exhale produce a soft ocean-wave or snoring sound.
Breathe through nose only; mouth closed.
Inhalation fills the chest expansively. Exhalation is controlled and complete.
Ratio: 1:2 (inhale:exhale) ideally.

respiratory: Increases airway resistance — improves lung efficiency
Beneficial for asthma and COPD
Warms the bronchial passages
endocrine: Mild thyroid stimulation via throat engagement
cardiovascular: Slows heart rate; reduces blood pressure

relative: cardiac disease — avoid strong breath retention

modifications.elderly: Easy to practice; chair seated; excellent for daily use.
modifications.obese: Chair seated; very accessible.
modifications.beginners: Focus on the throat sound first; ratio second.
modifications.pregnancy: Safe throughout; very beneficial.

instructions: Sit upright. Begin with a natural breath.
Inhale forcefully through the nose expanding the chest; exhale forcefully through the nose contracting the abdomen.
Both inhale and exhale are active and equal in Bhastrika (unlike Kapalbhati where only exhale is active).
Rhythm: 1 breath cycle per second. Start with 20 strokes per round.
After each round: take a deep inhalation, retain briefly, exhale slowly.

respiratory: Clears and strengthens entire respiratory tract
Highly effective for asthma, bronchitis, sinusitis
endocrine: Activates metabolism; stimulates adrenal glands
nervous: Energising; clears mental fog; improves alertness
cardiovascular: Increases circulation significantly

modifications.elderly: Mild version; 10 strokes per round; slow rhythm.
modifications.obese: Moderate pace; 15 strokes; short rounds.
modifications.beginners: 10–15 strokes per round; slow build.
modifications.pregnancy: Contraindicated.

instructions: Sit comfortably. Roll the tongue into a tube (like a straw); if unable, use Sheetkari instead.
Inhale deeply through the rolled tongue — the air passes over the tongue and is cooled.
Close mouth; hold briefly (optional).
Exhale slowly through both nostrils.
5–15 rounds.

respiratory: Cooling and moistening of airways
digestive: Reduces gastric heat; beneficial for acidity and GERD
nervous: Reduces excess heat in body and mind
cardiovascular: Reduces blood pressure

absolute: asthma — cold air may trigger bronchospasm

modifications.elderly: Chair seated; very accessible.
modifications.obese: Chair seated.
modifications.beginners: If tongue roll not possible, use Sheetkari (teeth slightly apart, inhale through teeth).
modifications.pregnancy: Safe; very beneficial for heat and discomfort.

instructions: Sit comfortably. Fold tongue back (or keep flat) and bring upper and lower teeth together lightly.
Part lips; inhale through the gap between the teeth with a hissing sound.
Close mouth; hold briefly; exhale through both nostrils.
5–15 rounds.

respiratory: Same as Sheetali — cooling and calming
digestive: Reduces gastric heat; beneficial for acidity
nervous: Cools and calms the nervous system
cardiovascular: Reduces blood pressure

modifications.elderly: Chair seated; easy to perform.
modifications.obese: Chair seated.
modifications.beginners: Use when tongue-rolling for Sheetali is not possible.
modifications.pregnancy: Safe.

instructions: Sit comfortably. Close left nostril with ring finger.
Inhale through right nostril (Surya/Sun nadi) slowly and deeply.
Close both nostrils; hold briefly.
Exhale through left nostril only.
Repeat: inhale right, exhale left. 5–15 rounds.

respiratory: Activates and balances respiratory patterns
endocrine: Activates sympathetic nervous system mildly — raises metabolic activity
nervous: Activating and warming — combats lethargy and cold

relative: pitta disorders — avoid in summer heat

modifications.elderly: Chair seated; gentle rhythm.
modifications.obese: Chair seated.
modifications.beginners: No retention initially; 5 rounds.
modifications.pregnancy: Use with caution; short practice only.

instructions: Sit comfortably. Close right nostril with thumb.
Inhale through left nostril (Chandra/Moon nadi) slowly and deeply.
Close both; hold briefly.
Exhale through right nostril only.
Repeat: inhale left, exhale right. 5–15 rounds.

endocrine: Activates parasympathetic system — calming and cooling
cardiovascular: Reduces blood pressure

modifications.elderly: Chair seated.
modifications.obese: Chair seated.
modifications.beginners: No retention; 5 rounds.
modifications.pregnancy: Safe and beneficial.

instructions: Sit in comfortable meditative posture. Spine erect.
Advanced version of Anulom Vilom — includes kumbhaka (breath retention).
Ratio: Inhale (1) : Hold (4) : Exhale (2) — e.g., 4:16:8 counts.
Inhale left nostril for 4 counts; hold both closed for 16 counts; exhale right for 8 counts.
Inhale right for 4; hold 16; exhale left for 8. This is one cycle.
10–20 cycles. Only after mastery of basic Anulom Vilom.

respiratory: Deep purification of all 72,000 nadis (energy channels)
Maximum respiratory efficiency
endocrine: Deep hormonal balancing
cardiovascular: Reduces BP significantly; improves cardiac function

modifications.elderly: Reduced retention time; lower count ratio.
modifications.obese: Accessible; chair seated; avoid prolonged retention.
modifications.beginners: Must master Anulom Vilom first; no retention initially.
modifications.pregnancy: Use without retention; ratio 1:0:2 only.

duration.hold: As per ratio in combined pranayama

instructions: Two types: Antar Kumbhaka (retention after inhalation) and Bahya Kumbhaka (retention after exhalation).
ANTAR: Inhale fully; close both nostrils; pause before exhaling. Start with 2–4 seconds; progress to 16+ seconds.
BAHYA: Exhale completely; close both nostrils; pause before inhaling.
NEVER hold the breath forcibly or to the point of gasping.
Always practiced within a pranayama framework — not as standalone.

respiratory: Increases CO2 tolerance; trains respiratory chemoreceptors
Improves lung function in asthma
digestive: Bahya Kumbhaka with bandhas stimulates digestive organs powerfully
endocrine: Stimulates endocrine glands through pressure changes
cardiovascular: Strengthens cardiac muscle with advanced practice

relative: anxiety — avoid until anxiety is well-controlled

modifications.elderly: Very brief retention only; always under supervision.
modifications.obese: Brief retention; no vigorous bandhas.
modifications.beginners: Only under direct guidance; start with 1–2 second holds.
modifications.pregnancy: Contraindicated.

instructions: Lie in Savasana or sit upright.
Place one hand on belly, one on chest.
Inhale in three stages: first belly rises (diaphragmatic), then ribs expand laterally, then upper chest lifts.
Exhale in reverse: upper chest falls, ribs contract, belly draws in.
No pauses; smooth and continuous flow.
10–20 cycles. Ideal as introductory breathing practice.

respiratory: Retrains full diaphragmatic breathing
Increases tidal volume by 200–300%
Foundation for all pranayama and singing/voice therapy
cardiovascular: Lowers blood pressure and heart rate

modifications.elderly: Supine position with knees bent; very accessible.
modifications.obese: Supine; excellent for retraining breathing pattern.
modifications.beginners: Best starting pranayama; no modifications needed.
modifications.pregnancy: Excellent throughout pregnancy.

instructions: Sit in comfortable meditative posture with spine erect.
After inhalation and during retention (kumbhaka): contract the perineal muscles (area between anus and genitals).
Lift and hold; breathe continues or is retained based on practice.
Gradually release on exhalation.
Practice in isolation first: rhythmically contract and release the perineum 10–20 times.

respiratory: Seals prana in the body — used with pranayama
endocrine: Stimulates sacral plexus and reproductive glands
nervous: Awakens Muladhara chakra; grounds the nervous system
urogenital: Highly beneficial for urinary incontinence, pelvic organ prolapse
Benefits prostate disorders
Treats chronic constipation

relative: pregnancy — modified version only with supervision

modifications.elderly: Isolated contractions; no full kumbhaka required.
modifications.obese: Seated; accessible without complex posture.
modifications.beginners: Practice isolation contractions before integrating with pranayama.
modifications.pregnancy: Gentle perineal contractions only; no strong retention.

instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width. Bend forward slightly, hands on knees.
Exhale completely through the mouth.
After exhale (Bahya Kumbhaka): draw the abdomen sharply upward and inward — under the rib cage.
Hold this 'hollow belly' for 5–15 seconds.
Relax abdomen, then slowly inhale.
Repeat 5–10 rounds on an empty stomach only.

respiratory: Massages diaphragm and lungs
digestive: Powerful massage for all abdominal organs
Excellent for constipation and sluggish digestion
endocrine: Stimulates adrenal glands and solar plexus
nervous: Activates Manipura chakra; increases confidence and energy

modifications.elderly: Very gentle; limited contraction; no vigorous pull.
modifications.obese: Accessible; excellent for abdominal toning.
modifications.beginners: Learn on empty stomach; master Bahya Kumbhaka first.
modifications.pregnancy: Contraindicated.

instructions: A dynamic sequence of 12 postures performed in a flowing cycle:
1. Pranamasana (Prayer) — exhale
2. Hasta Uttanasana (Raised Arms) — inhale
3. Uttanasana (Forward Fold) — exhale
4. Ashwa Sanchalanasana (Equestrian right leg back) — inhale
5. Dandasana/Plank — hold
6. Ashtanga Namaskar (8-point prostration) — exhale
7. Bhujangasana (Cobra) — inhale
8. Adho Mukha Svanasana (Downward Dog) — exhale
9. Ashwa Sanchalanasana (Equestrian left leg forward) — inhale
10. Uttanasana — exhale
11. Hasta Uttanasana — inhale
12. Pranamasana — exhale
Left leg back = one round; right leg back = second round.

musculoskeletal: Full-body workout — all major muscle groups activated
respiratory: Synchronised breath-movement — maximal respiratory training
Alternating chest compression and expansion
endocrine: Stimulates entire endocrine system including thyroid, adrenals, pancreas, gonads
nervous: Balances ANS; energises and calms alternately
cardiovascular: Excellent cardiovascular conditioning — equivalent to moderate-intensity aerobic exercise
Reduces cholesterol and improves circulation
urogenital: Stimulates pelvic organs

relative: hypertension — slow pace; avoid holding breath

modifications.elderly: Slow pace; use chair for support in standing poses; skip inversions.
modifications.obese: Start with 2–3 slow rounds; chair-supported variations.
modifications.beginners: Learn each posture individually before attempting the flow.
modifications.pregnancy: Modified prenatal Sun Salutation — avoid Bhujangasana; replace with Balasana.

instructions: Begin in Tadasana. Shift weight onto the left foot.
Hinge forward from the hip, simultaneously lifting the right leg behind.
Body forms a T-shape: torso, arms, and right leg parallel to floor.
Flex the right foot strongly; keep hips squared to the ground.
Arms can extend forward, rest on hips, or hold a chair for support.
Gaze slightly forward and down to maintain neck alignment.
Hold steadily for 20–40 seconds; release slowly. Repeat on left.

musculoskeletal: Strengthens gluteals, hamstrings, ankle stabilisers and thoracic erectors
Improves hip extension and spinal extension simultaneously
neurological: Develops unilateral proprioception and vestibular coordination
rehabilitative: Key pose for post-fracture and post-stroke lower limb re-strengthening

absolute: Active lumbar disc herniation with sciatica

modifications.beginner: Hands on chair or wall; lift leg only to comfortable height
modifications.elderly: Face wall, hands on wall for support
modifications.post-surgery: Lift leg only 10–20° until strength returns

instructions: Stand with feet 4 feet apart. Right foot turns out 90°; left foot in 15°.
Bend right knee to 90° (knee over ankle). Left leg stays straight and strong.
Exhale, extend torso over right thigh; place right forearm on right thigh or fingertips on floor.
Left arm extends over left ear, creating one long diagonal line from left foot to left fingertips.
Rotate chest toward ceiling; gaze up at upper arm or straight ahead.
Keep right knee pressing outward (do not let it cave in).
Hold for 30–60 seconds. Inhale to rise; repeat left side.

musculoskeletal: Stretches groins, hamstrings, calves and lateral thorax
digestive: Deep lateral compression stimulates ascending/descending colon — aids constipation
lymphatic: Stretches axillary and inguinal lymph node regions
reproductive: Gentle pelvic compression benefits PCOS and menstrual cramping

modifications.beginner: Forearm on thigh instead of hand to floor
modifications.pregnancy: Use block on outside of foot; widen stance
modifications.low back pain: Do not rotate chest upward; keep torso neutral

instructions: Stand with feet 3 feet apart. Right foot points forward; left foot turns out 45–60°.
Square hips to face forward by drawing right hip back and left hip forward.
Place hands in reverse Namaste behind back (or hold elbows behind).
Inhale, lengthen the spine. Exhale, hinge from hips and fold over the right leg.
Keep both legs straight and both hips level.
Rest the chin/nose on the shin if flexibility allows; otherwise hold at shin or knee.
Hold, breathing evenly. Inhale to rise slowly; step feet together; repeat left.

musculoskeletal: Deep hamstring and hip flexor stretch
Opens shoulder girdle in Paschima Namaskar variation
digestive: Abdominal compression on thigh stimulates peristalsis
postural: Reverses anterior pelvic tilt; counteracts prolonged sitting posture

absolute: Severe osteoporosis with vertebral fracture risk
relative: Hypertension (use chair, keep head above heart)

modifications.tight hamstrings: Slight bend in front knee; hands on blocks
modifications.shoulder injury: Hands on hips instead of behind back
modifications.pregnancy: Hands on blocks; fold only to horizontal

instructions: Stand in Tadasana, feet hip-width or together.
Inhale, raise both arms overhead, lengthen the spine.
Exhale slowly, hinge forward from the hips keeping the back flat initially.
Let the upper body hang down; allow gravity to decompress the spine.
Place palms flat under the feet (toes grip the wrists) or hold ankles.
With each exhale, allow deeper relaxation into the pose; do not force.
Bend knees if hamstrings are tight — a flat back is more important than straight legs.
To release: bend knees, press feet down and slowly unroll vertebra by vertebra.

digestive: Splanchnic circulation boost improves liver, pancreas and intestinal function
musculoskeletal: Stretches entire posterior chain — hamstrings, calves, spinal erectors
circulatory: Inverted position aids venous return from lower limbs
neurological: Increased blood supply to brain; calming effect on sympathetic nervous system

absolute: Lumbar disc herniation with acute radiculopathy

modifications.beginners: Keep knees generously bent; rest hands on shins
modifications.back pain: Hands on chair; hinge from hips only to horizontal
modifications.pregnancy: Wide stance; fold only until belly allows; use chair

instructions: Stand in Tadasana. Shift weight onto the left foot.
Bend right knee, lift right foot behind. Reach right hand back to grasp the right ankle or foot.
Raise left arm forward and up for balance.
Kick the right foot backward and upward into the hand, lifting the thigh parallel to floor.
Simultaneously hinge the torso slightly forward; maintain balance.
Drishti (gaze): fixed point at eye level on left fingertips.
Hold for 20–30 seconds breathing steadily. Release slowly; repeat on left.

musculoskeletal: Opens chest and shoulders; stretches quadriceps and hip flexors
Strengthens standing leg, core and spinal extensors
psychological: Builds courage, grace and concentration — beneficial in depression and low self-esteem

relative: High blood pressure (do not hold breath)

modifications.beginner: Use strap around back foot; other hand on wall
modifications.balance issue: Practise near wall or with chair

instructions: Sit in Vajrasana (kneeling pose).
Make fists with both hands: thumbs tucked inside the four fingers.
Place fists on the abdomen, pressing firmly on either side of the navel (targeting the pancreatic region).
Exhale completely, pressing fists deeper into the abdomen.
Hold the breath outside (bahir kumbhaka) while leaning forward and pressing.
Hold for 10–15 seconds (or as comfortable); inhale and sit upright.
Relax in Vajrasana for 3–5 breaths; repeat 3–5 rounds.
Variation B (wide frog): kneel with knees wide apart, hips sinking toward floor.

endocrine: Direct mechanical stimulation of pancreatic beta cells — reduces blood glucose
Stimulates adrenal glands and liver
digestive: Massages liver, spleen and intestines; relieves gas and bloating
metabolic: Increases splanchnic circulation — enhances metabolic organ function
reproductive: Pelvic compression stimulates ovarian and uterine circulation — beneficial in PCOS

modifications.knee pain: Place folded blanket under knees in Vajrasana
modifications.hypertension: Perform without kumbhaka; gentle abdominal pressure only
modifications.DM routine: 3–5 rounds before meals twice daily for best glycaemic effect

instructions: Sit in Dandasana (legs straight). Bend the right knee, place right foot flat on floor beside left knee.
Inhale, lengthen the spine. Exhale, twist the torso to the right.
Wrap left arm around the right knee or place left elbow outside right knee.
Right hand rests on floor behind the right hip for support.
With each exhale, rotate further without straining the spine.
Gaze over the right shoulder. Keep both sitting bones grounded.
Hold 30 seconds; inhale to centre. Repeat to the left side.
Simpler than Ardha Matsyendrasana — ideal for beginners, elderly and diabetics.

endocrine: Lateral spinal twist compresses and releases pancreas — stimulates insulin secretion
musculoskeletal: Mobilises thoracic and lumbar spine; relieves morning back stiffness
neurological: Traction effect on sciatic nerve roots — mild sciatica relief

relative: Pregnancy (twist only upper thorax; no abdominal compression)

modifications.beginner: Keep both legs straight; simply rotate torso with hand support behind
modifications.knee pain: Sit on folded blanket to ease knee flexion
modifications.pregnancy: Rotate above navel only; no compression of lower abdomen

instructions: Sit on the floor with legs extended. Bend the left knee; place the left heel at the perineum (root chakra area).
Bend the right knee; place the right heel against or above the pubic bone.
Rest both knees on the floor (use props if knees float).
Lengthen the spine; allow the pelvis to tilt slightly forward.
Rest hands on knees in Chin Mudra or Jnana Mudra.
Close eyes; relax the face, jaw and shoulders.
Breathe naturally; hold for the duration of meditation or pranayama practice.
Alternate leg position each session for balanced hip opening.

neurological: Heel pressure at perineum stimulates Mooladhara chakra and pelvic floor nerves
Promotes parasympathetic dominance — ideal for pranayama and meditation
cardiovascular: Sustained practice reduces resting heart rate and blood pressure
endocrine: Activates hypnophrenic (tranquil) state; reduces cortisol
urogenital: Pelvic floor activation benefits pelvic floor weakness and mild incontinence

modifications.tight hips: Sit on folded blanket 5–10 cm height; use blocks under both knees
modifications.knee pain: Practise Sukhasana until hip flexibility improves
modifications.elderly: Chair seated with feet flat — maintain mudra and spinal alignment

instructions: Sit in Padmasana or Sukhasana. Clasp hands behind the back (fingers interlaced).
Inhale deeply, sitting tall.
Exhale, hinge forward from the hips bringing the forehead toward the floor.
Simultaneously raise clasped hands upward behind you (shoulder stretch).
In Padmasana: the knees press down; the ankles create direct pressure on abdominal organs.
Breathe gently in the final position. Hold for 30 seconds to 2 minutes.
Inhale to return upright slowly. Release hands. Repeat 3 rounds.

endocrine: Abdominal compression directly stimulates pancreas and adrenal glands
digestive: Massages all abdominal organs; relieves gas and improves gut motility
cardiovascular: Vagal stimulation slows heart rate; beneficial in mild tachycardia
psychological: Forward fold activates parasympathetic system — profound calming effect in anxiety

relative: Shoulder injury (use strap instead of clasped hands)

modifications.tight shoulders: Hold a strap or belt between hands behind back
modifications.beginner: Practise in Sukhasana; fold only to horizontal

instructions: Sit in Dandasana. Bend the right knee, placing right foot flat on floor close to right sitting bone.
Left leg remains straight; flex left foot strongly.
Inhale, lengthen the spine. Exhale, rotate the torso gently right.
Wrap right arm behind the back; left arm comes around the outside of the right knee.
Clasp hands if possible behind the back; otherwise use a strap.
Inhale to lengthen; exhale to rotate further without forcing.
Keep both sitting bones grounded. Hold 30–60 seconds.
Inhale to release; switch to left side.

digestive: Lateral abdominal compression on bent-knee side stimulates ascending or descending colon and kidney
musculoskeletal: Lengthens hamstring of straight leg; opens shoulder girdle
hepatorenal: Compression-release action stimulates liver (right side) and spleen (left side)

modifications.shoulder limit: Use strap between hands instead of binding
modifications.tight hamstring: Sit on blanket; slight bend in straight leg permitted
modifications.beginner: Only rotate torso without binding; place opposite hand on knee

instructions: Lie prone. Bring elbows directly under shoulders; forearms flat on floor, parallel to each other.
Legs straight back, hip-width apart; tops of feet on floor.
Inhale, press forearms down and lift the chest and head off the floor.
Keep pubic bone pressing into floor; do not harden the buttocks.
Lengthen the tailbone toward heels; lift the sternum forward.
Hold 1–3 minutes with slow nasal breathing.
Excellent gentler alternative to Bhujangasana for disc patients.

musculoskeletal: Gentle lumbar extension centralises disc nucleus pulposus — McKenzie principle
Strengthens deep spinal extensors with minimal load
respiratory: Passive chest opening expands anterior thorax; increases tidal volume
neurological: Prone position with thoracic extension stimulates sympathetic chain mildly — energising
psychological: Opening the heart space in prone — gentle mood uplift in fatigue and mild depression

relative: Cervical spondylosis (keep head neutral, don't hyperextend neck)

modifications.disc patient: Therapeutic protocol: 10 repetitions × 10 sec holds, 3–4 times daily in acute phase
modifications.cervical issue: Keep gaze at floor; do not hyperextend the neck
modifications.wrist pain: Make soft fists; lean on them rather than flat palms

instructions: From a kneeling push-up position, exhale and lower the body.
Touch floor sequentially: toes, knees, chest, chin (8 points: two feet, two knees, two hands, chest, chin).
Hips remain slightly raised; spine is in slight extension at the thoracic level.
Elbows press close to the body (not flared out).
Chin (not forehead) touches the floor; throat is compressed against the chest (jalandhara-like).
Hold for 5–10 seconds with natural breathing.
Push up into Bhujangasana or Adho Mukha Svanasana to transition forward.

endocrine: Chin-to-chest position applies mild pressure to thyroid — beneficial in hypothyroidism protocol
rehabilitative: Lower-load alternative to Chaturanga for beginners and post-surgery

absolute: Wrist fracture or carpal tunnel severe

modifications.beginner: Practise the lowering movement slowly before holding
modifications.upper body weakness: Lower one body segment at a time

instructions: Lie supine, legs together, arms alongside body palms down.
Inhale, raise both legs to 30° off the floor; hold 10–15 seconds.
Continue raising to 60°; hold 10–15 seconds.
Raise to 90° (vertical); hold 10–15 seconds.
Lower back through 60° and 30° with the same controlled holds.
Keep the lower back pressed into the floor throughout (lumbar protection).
One-leg variation: alternate legs for beginners with weak core.
Complete 3–5 full rounds.

musculoskeletal: Strengthens hip flexors, lower abdominals and lumbar erectors
digestive: Pressure on lower abdomen at 30–60° stimulates sigmoid colon and rectum — relieves constipation
endocrine: Lower abdominal compression stimulates pancreatic tail — aids glycaemic control
urogenital: Tones pelvic floor and bladder musculature

absolute: Acute lumbar disc herniation (keep knees bent if needed)

modifications.weak core: One leg at a time; other foot flat on floor
modifications.disc issue: Knees slightly bent; do not lower below 45°
modifications.hypertension: Breathe normally; do not hold breath during holds

instructions: Lie supine with arms alongside body, palms pressing floor.
Inhale; using abdominal and hip flexor muscles, raise both legs together to 90°.
Do not swing or use momentum; control the lift.
Toes point toward ceiling; legs remain straight and together.
Hold for 20–60 seconds breathing steadily.
Exhale, lower legs slowly (do not drop them) back to floor.
Distinction from Halasana: legs remain vertical (90°) — does NOT go overhead.
Safer and more accessible than full Halasana; indicated for beginners.

digestive: Vertical leg position redirects venous blood from legs into abdominal cavity — improves gut circulation
endocrine: Abdominal compression at the base stimulates pancreas and adrenals
circulatory: Assists venous return from legs — prevents varicose veins worsening
musculoskeletal: Strengthens lower abdominals and hip flexors without cervical stress of full Halasana

relative: Lumbar disc herniation (support with hands under sacrum)

modifications.weak core: Bend knees or use single-leg version
modifications.low back pain: Place hands under the sacrum for support
modifications.hypertension: Do not hold breath; breathe continuously

instructions: Lie on back. Exhale, draw both knees into the chest.
Inhale; lift feet toward ceiling, opening knees wider than torso.
Take hold of the outer edges of both feet (or ankles or backs of thighs).
Flex both feet so soles face the ceiling.
Gently draw knees toward armpits; keep sacrum flat on floor.
Rock gently side to side to massage the sacrum and lumbar spine.
Hold 1–3 minutes; breathe deeply and allow the hips to release passively.

musculoskeletal: Passive hip flexor and inner groin stretch without active load
Gentle traction of lumbar spine and decompression of sacroiliac joints
neurological: Parasympathetic activation — ideal as cooldown or before Savasana
rehabilitative: Low-risk restorative pose suitable for most clinical populations including elderly

relative: Neck injury (keep head on floor, use pillow)

modifications.tight hips: Hold behind thighs instead of feet
modifications.limited range: Keep only one knee bent at a time (Eka Pada variation)

instructions: Lie supine. Left leg remains flat and straight on floor.
Bend right knee; loop a strap around the right foot's arch.
Inhale, straighten right leg toward ceiling, holding the strap.
Variation A: leg vertical — hamstring and calf stretch.
Variation B: lower leg to the right (abduction) keeping left hip grounded — inner thigh and IT band stretch.
Variation C: cross leg to the left (adduction with rotation) — piriformis and sciatic nerve stretch.
Hold each variation 30–60 seconds. Return to centre; switch legs.

musculoskeletal: Comprehensive hamstring, calf, adductor and abductor stretching in a single pose
neurological: Neural mobilisation of sciatic nerve — highly effective in sciatica management
urogenital: Hip external rotation variation (Variation C) stretches piriformis — benefits prostate congestion

relative: Hip replacement (check surgical restrictions with orthopaedic)

modifications.sciatica: Keep elevated leg at 45°; gradually increase over days
modifications.no strap: Hold behind thigh rather than foot

instructions: Begin in Phalakasana (Plank Pose).
Shift weight onto the right hand and outer edge of right foot.
Stack the left foot on top of the right; left arm extends toward ceiling.
Body forms one straight diagonal line from head to heel.
Press right hand actively into floor; lift right hip away from floor.
Engage core, obliques and gluteals throughout.
Gaze at left hand or forward. Hold 15–30 seconds.
Return to Plank; repeat on left side.

musculoskeletal: Strengthens oblique abdominals, gluteus medius, shoulder rotator cuff and wrist stabilisers simultaneously
rehabilitative: Strengthening the concave-side obliques in mild scoliosis may reduce curve progression
metabolic: High-activation isometric exercise — burns calories and improves metabolic rate

modifications.beginner: Lower knee to floor (from stacked-feet to knee-down position)
modifications.scoliosis: Focus on strengthening the weaker concave side; physician guidance required

instructions: Begin in Phalakasana (Plank). Elbows press against the rib cage.
Inhale to prepare. Exhale, lower the body as one rigid unit.
Stop when upper arms are parallel to floor (elbows at 90°).
Body remains in a straight line — hips do not drop or pike.
Shoulders must not dip below elbow level (prevents shoulder impingement).
Hold 5–20 seconds; push back to Plank or up to Urdhva Mukha Svanasana.
Beginners: lower knees first; build to full expression over weeks.

musculoskeletal: Eccentric loading of pectorals, triceps and anterior deltoids
Activates serratus anterior — key for scapular stability and preventing winging
core: Isometric transversus abdominis and rectus abdominis activation
metabolic: High-intensity isometric — significant caloric expenditure when included in Surya Namaskar sequences

absolute: Wrist fracture or severe carpal tunnel

modifications.beginner: Knees on floor; focus on keeping elbows tucked
modifications.shoulder rehab: Do not lower past 45° until cleared by physiotherapist

instructions: Lie supine, legs straight, arms alongside body.
Inhale deeply. Exhale, simultaneously lift head, shoulders and both legs 30–45° off the floor.
Arms stretch forward parallel to floor, pointing toward the feet.
Body forms a V or banana shape, with only the sacrum/lower back on the floor.
Gaze at the toes. Hold for 10–30 seconds with normal breathing.
Inhale; slowly lower head and legs simultaneously back to floor.
Note: This is the SUPINE version. Navasana (yoga_017) is the seated/boat version.
Repeat 3–5 rounds with 10-second rest between rounds.

musculoskeletal: Simultaneous hip flexor and abdominal activation — integrative core exercise
endocrine: Sustained abdominal tension stimulates pancreatic blood flow — beneficial in DM
digestive: Abdominal wall engagement massages underlying viscera

relative: Neck weakness (support head with hands behind occiput)

modifications.neck weakness: Keep one hand behind the head for support
modifications.disc problem: Raise one leg at a time; keep opposite foot on floor
modifications.elderly: Lift only legs or only upper body separately before combining

instructions: Begin in Adho Mukha Svanasana near a wall. Hands 6 inches from the wall.
Stagger feet; take a strong step in with dominant leg and kick the other leg up.
Use the wall to support heels; press actively through the palms.
Engage the core strongly; stack hips over shoulders over wrists.
Press shoulder blades apart (protract scapulae); do not lock elbows.
Hold 10–30 seconds; come down one leg at a time, slowly.
Only attempt freestanding after mastering wall version.

musculoskeletal: Maximum shoulder girdle, wrist and core strengthening
circulatory: Complete venous and lymphatic reversal of lower body drainage
neurological: Activates concentration, vestibular coordination and fear-response management
psychological: Profound confidence building; energising — specifically indicated in depression protocol

relative: Anxiety (use only with experienced teacher guidance)
